item.title: _check_groom_progress uses md5sum which fails on macOS without coreutils
item.intent: Ensure groom progress monitoring works on macOS by using a portable hashing command, so backlog change detection during grooming is not broken for macOS users.
item.description: In _check_groom_progress(), backlog file changes are detected using md5sum (lines 958 and 969 in the current codebase). md5sum is a GNU coreutils command that is not available on macOS by default. macOS provides 'md5' (different interface) or 'shasum'/'sha256sum' (different output format). When md5sum is missing, the command silently fails (cur_hash will be empty) and no changes are ever detected, so the progress monitor never reports backlog updates during groom sessions. The fix should use the same portability pattern as verify_checksum() — try sha256sum first, then shasum, then fall back to a simple wc-c checksum if neither is available.
steps: Locate _check_groom_progress() in .aishore/aishore and find the two md5sum calls that compute cur_hash
Replace both md5sum calls with a portable hash helper that tries sha256sum, then shasum -a 256, then cksum as a last resort
Define a local _file_hash function or inline the portable hash command using the same approach as verify_checksum(): if command -v sha256sum, elif command -v shasum, else cksum
Verify the replacement produces a stable hash for the same file content (cksum is acceptable as a fallback since we only need change detection, not cryptographic security)
Test on macOS: run 'aishore groom' and confirm that backlog update messages are printed during the session
acceptanceCriteria: _check_groom_progress correctly detects backlog file changes on macOS when only md5 or shasum is available (not md5sum)
_check_groom_progress correctly detects backlog file changes on Linux where sha256sum or md5sum are available
No regression: progress messages are still printed when backlog files change during groom sessions
The portable hash approach does not generate errors or warnings when md5sum is absent
